Off Calendar Dec-24-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Marquis Owens, Esq., counsel for Francois Wise, Executor (appearing by telephone).

The Court recites the matters now before the Court and notes the recent filings in response to the Examiner's Notes. Mr. Owens advises the Court of the filing of waivers, verifications and a Probate Code Sec. 13100 declaration.

The Court takes the matter under submission with the stated intention to grant the Petition upon review.

Off Calendar Oct-16-2018 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-18-2018 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Owens Marquis for Petitioner.

Mr. Marquis stated that they are not ready to distribute at this point, that he filed a petition for full letters of authority with a hearing date of December 4th and requested that the hearing date be moved up. Mr. Marquis also stated that there is one significant piece of property, that he will file a petition for the Administrator to purchase the home, that he wants to avoid paying another filing fee and asked the Court to wait on this petition. The Court advised Mr. Marquis that he needs to file an ex parte application to advance the hearing date and stated that nothing is happening with this petition. The Petition for Final Distribution is off calendar. Status on filing of a petition for approval of report on status of administration is set for October 16, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-24-2018 Continued To Sep-18-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Francois Wise appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.

The Court noted that it is still waiting for response to the Probate Examiner's notes before it can move forward on the petition. Mr. Wise requested a continuance to February 2019 stating that he has substituted in an Attorney and he is getting him up to speed, and that they are in the process of obtaining a loan because some of the siblings want out. The Court informs Mr. Wise that it will not continue the matter that far out, that he can withdraw this petition and the Court can set out a new status date for close of the estate. Mr. Wise stated that he hired an Attorney last week, that he is getting the paper over to the Attorney and that the Attorney will be substituting in. The Petition for Final Distribution is continued to September 18, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the Attorney to make an appearance, respond to the Examiner's notes and to determine if this petition is viable to going forward or not.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Aug-17-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Francois Wise, petitioner pro per (by CourtCall); Vandy Clark.

The Court inquired if Mr. Wise was prepared to assume the duties of executor and he stated that he was. The Court asked Ms. Clark if she had any objection to the petitioon, and she responded that she did not. The Court stated that he would grant the petition with the same limitation on authority as granted to the original executor. The Court granted the petition (subject to review and modification of the order). The status hearing re closing of estate was ordered continued from June 21, 2017 to February 20, 2018.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing An Inventory & Appraisal
Off Calendar Dec-16-2015 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jun-19-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Alanna Jane Wise in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that this is a petition for probate, that it is ready for action, that the court does not typically appoint a Pro Per, that the procedures regarding the estate can be complicated, that there is an issue of conflict of interest of Ms. Wise living in the property rent free and that there may be objections by other beneficiaries. Ms. Wise stated that she will be obtaining an attorney, that she lived in the house because she was taking care of her father, that the beneficiaries consented to her staying there, that she will be moving out and making repairs to the property and that they would like to keep the property in the family. The court recommended that Ms. Wise obtain counsel, that it trusts the family is getting along, that these matters can create issues within the family, that an attorney would be able to let everyone know what their rights are and that everyone understands what is involved, that it is inclined to grant the petition with limited authority, that Ms. Wise would need to get court authorization to sell the property and that status dates will be set. The Court granted the petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary (subject to review of the order)with limited authority. Status of filing and Inventory & Appraisal is set for December 16, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status of filing a Petition for Final Distribution is set for June 18, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Subsequent to the hearing it was realized that the status date of June 18, 2017 was not proper and a new date of June 21, 2017 was issued. The clerk telephoned the executor to advise her of the change. L.Gomez

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
